basics.name: Sabaat Haroon
basics.label: PhD Candidate in Computer Science
basics.summary: PhD candidate at Virginia Tech researching software engineering, fault localization, and data-intensive scalable computing. Over 4 years of experience with Apache Spark.

work.name: Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State University
work.summary: Supervised by Dr. Muhammad Ali Gulzar. Research on fault localizability of LLMs, DeSQL (interactive SQL debugging for Spark), and AutoSQLDebug.
highlights: Assessing the Impact of Code Changes on the Fault Localizability of Large Language Models (IEEE ICST 2026)
DeSQL: Interactive Debugging of SQL in Data-Intensive Scalable Computing (ICSE 2024)
Towards cost-effective and resource-aware aggregation at Edge for Federated Learning (IEEE BigData 2023)

work.name: Virginia Tech
work.summary: Delivered recitations and office hours; mentored teams; graded assignments and projects with detailed technical feedback.

work.name: i2c Inc.
work.summary: Deployed enterprise applications. Jira, full SDLC, testing approaches, Kanban, security (SQL Injection, XSS, DDoS).

highlights: Research in fault localization, LLMs, data-intensive systems

publications.name: Assessing the Impact of Code Changes on the Fault Localizability of Large Language Models
publications.summary: Evaluates debugging capability of LLMs under code evolution over 750,013 fault-localization tasks; LLMs fail to localize same fault after non-functional changes in 78% of cases.

publications.name: DeSQL: Interactive Debugging of SQL in Data-Intensive Scalable Computing
publications.summary: Automated, fine-grained query debugging for Apache Spark SQL; 7.4× less debugging time than naïve approach.

publications.name: Towards cost-effective and resource-aware aggregation at Edge for Federated Learning
publications.summary: With A.F. Khan, Y. Li, X. Wang, et al.
